text:
Your working directory is a checkout of the real react-hook-form repository at the commit where the bug below reproduces. Find and fix the bug in the library source.

Bug: when a form is created in the disabled state (`useForm({ disabled: true })`), calling `setValue(name, value, { shouldDirty: true })` fails to mark anything dirty. After registering a field and then programmatically setting a value that differs from its default with `shouldDirty: true`, `getFieldState(name).isDirty`, `formState.isDirty`, and `formState.dirtyFields` all incorrectly stay in their pristine state — even though the new value clearly differs from the default. Conversely, programmatically setting the field back to its default value on a disabled form should clear that dirty state again. The root cause is that the dirty-tracking path treats a disabled form as 'never track dirtiness' and swallows the update entirely. The correct behavior is that an explicit programmatic dirty request (`setValue` with `shouldDirty: true`) is honored even while the form is disabled, while ordinary user interaction (typing/blur) on a disabled form still does NOT track dirtiness. Fix the library source (under `src/`); do not just add workarounds in test files.

Environment notes: dependencies are already installed (via `npm install -g pnpm@10.34.5 && CYPRESS_INSTALL_BINARY=0 pnpm install --no-frozen-lockfile --ignore-scripts`). Run tests with `node_modules/.bin/jest --config ./scripts/jest/jest.config.js src/__tests__/useForm/formState.test.tsx`.

message: `--dangerously-bypass-hook-trust` is enabled. Enabled hooks may run without review for this invocation.
`--dangerously-bypass-hook-trust` is enabled. Enabled hooks may run without review for this invocation.

text: I’ll trace the dirty-state update path in `src/`, identify where the disabled-form guard suppresses explicit `setValue` dirty updates, patch the smallest library change, and run the targeted Jest suite.

input.command: /bin/bash -lc "sed -n '390,510p' src/logic/createFormControl.ts; sed -n '730,825p' src/logic/createFormControl.ts; sed -n '1250,1300p' src/logic/createFormControl.ts; rg -n \"updateTouchAndDirty\\(\" src/logic/createFormControl.ts src/__tests__/useForm/formState.test.tsx"

/bin/bash -lc "rg -n \"useForm\\(\\{["'^}]*disabled|disabled: true|disabled.*shouldDirty|shouldDirty.*disabled" src/__tests__ | head -100; sed -n '"'1020,1080p' src/logic/createFormControl.ts; sed -n '130,170p' src/useForm.ts"
/bin/bash -lc "rg -n \"_getDirty\" src | head -80; sed -n '880,925p' src/types/form.ts; sed -n '1,120p' src/logic/createFormControl.ts"

/bin/bash -lc "rg -n \"export type GetIsDirty|type GetIsDirty\" src/types/form.ts; sed -n '1,35p' src/types/form.ts; sed -n '1680,1710p' src/logic/createFormControl.ts; sed -n '1825,1850p' src/logic/createFormControl.ts"
/bin/bash -lc "sed -n '800,820p' src/types/form.ts; git branch --show-current; git remote -v | head"

text: The relevant path is `updateTouchAndDirty`, which currently encloses both dirty and touched handling in `if (!_options.disabled)`. I’m preserving that suppression for ordinary events while allowing the `shouldDirty` branch, and I’m also removing the disabled short-circuit from the shared dirtiness comparison so the explicit update is not immediately collapsed back to pristine.

text: The targeted suite passes (35 tests). The patch now lets `shouldDirty: true` enter dirty tracking while disabled, while `onChange`/blur calls still remain excluded because they do not pass `shouldDirty`; the shared dirty comparison also now reflects actual values, allowing dirty state to clear when reset to the default.

input.command: /bin/bash -lc 'git diff --check && git status --short && git diff --stat && git diff -- src/logic/createFormControl.ts'

text:
Fixed in [src/logic/createFormControl.ts](/home/user/workspace/src/logic/createFormControl.ts:444).

- Explicit `setValue(..., { shouldDirty: true })` now tracks dirtiness on disabled forms.
- Resetting to the default clears dirty state.
- Disabled user input/blur still does not track dirtiness.

Validation: targeted Jest suite passes — 35 tests.
